h2: How to prove your language ability
What to include in your application
How to find your language level based on your test results

sections.heading: National Occupational Classification 2021
items: On November 16, 2022, we switched to the 2021 version of the National Occupational Classification (NOC) .
Use the NOC 2016 page if you applied for a job offer on or before November 15, 2022 .

sections.heading: On this page
bullets: How to prove your language ability
How to find your language level based on your test results

sections.heading: How to prove your language ability
items: To prove your language skills in English or French, you need to take an approved language test. To do this:
After you get the results of your test, compare them to the charts below to see what Canadian Language Benchmark (CLB) level you have.
bullets: schedule your test with an approved agency

sections.heading: What to include in your application
items: If you meet the minimum score in all 4 abilities, include a copy of the test results with your application.
These results must be less than 2 years old when you apply. Keep a copy of them for your records and future use.
The minimum score depends on the NOC 2021 TEER category of your job offer:
bullets: CLB 5 for job offer in TEER 0, 1, 2 or 3
CLB 4 for job offer in TEER 4

sections.heading: Language tests we accept
items: You can take any of these approved language tests:

sections.heading: English
bullets: CELPIP: Canadian English Language Proficiency Index Program You need to take the CELPIP-General test.
You need to take the CELPIP-General test.
IELTS: International English Language Testing System You need to take the IELTS General Training test.
You need to take the IELTS General Training test.
PTE: Pearson Test of English You need to take the PTE Core test.
You need to take the PTE Core test.

sections.heading: French
bullets: TEF Canada: Test d’évaluation de français
TCF Canada : Test de connaissance du français
